params.name: The Canterbury Tales
params.description: This collection of short stories, totaling over seventy thousand words, presents the multifaceted nature of medieval English society through twenty-four tales told by a group of pilgrims on their journey. From knights to nuns, merchants to scholars, their stories cover a variety of themes including love, adventure, religion, and satire. Chaucer skillfully uses humor and satire, giving the work both literary depth and entertainment value. The novel is not only popular in its native England but ha …
